text.title: openai-api Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for openai-api as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Tool call response missing `tool_call_id` matching the request.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: OpenAI requires each tool result to reference the exact `tool_call_id` from the request. Mismatches cause HTTP 400 errors. Always map results back to the call ID.

failure-mode.description: Mixing Responses API and Chat Completions in the same conversation flow.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The two APIs manage conversation history differently. Responses API stores history server-side; Chat Completions requires developer-managed history. Mixing them produces inconsistent context.

failure-mode.description: Submitting a Batch API job and then waiting synchronously for results.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Batch API results arrive asynchronously (up to 24h). Synchronous waiting will time out. Use a separate completion-handler agent or poll the batch status endpoint.

failure-mode.description: Using `finish_reason: "length"` response downstream without checking truncation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A truncated response means the model was cut off mid-output. Passing incomplete data downstream silently corrupts all dependent states. Always check finish_reason.
